---
title: "Gen1: Selective Prediction"
subtitle: "Early 2025 | AA Snowflake Internal Data | Deprecated"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: false
    page-layout: full
    smooth-scroll: true
---

```{=html}
<nav style="margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
  <small><a href="index.html">Home</a> › <a href="research-overview-en.html">Research Overview</a> › Gen1</small>
  <br>
  <a href="research-overview-en.html" style="display:inline-block;margin-top:0.5rem;padding:0.3rem 0.8rem;border:1px solid #ccc;border-radius:4px;text-decoration:none;font-size:0.9rem;">← Back to Research Overview</a>
</nav>
```

> **Status: Deprecated.** The core feature ETD has fatal data leakage; all analysis results are invalid.

---

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Overview {#overview}

### Research Objective

*"When NOT to Use Flight Delay Prediction Models"*

The core concept is **Selective Prediction** — allowing the model to actively refuse predictions when uncertain, rather than forcing incorrect answers.

| Item | Details |
|------|---------|
| Dataset | American Airlines Snowflake Internal Data |
| Flights | 2.7M (full fleet) |
| Label | Arrival delay ≥ 15 minutes (`delayed_15`) |
| Temporal Split | Train 2020–2023 / Val 2024H1 / Test 2024H2 |
| Scale | 30 Python scripts (code_001 → code_030) |
| Development | ~14 days rapid prototype |

### Core Technical Framework

- **Selective Classification** — Reject low-confidence predictions, only answer when confident
- **Model Calibration** — Temperature Scaling for probability calibration (T = 1.050)
- **Cost-based Abstention** — Find optimal rejection threshold based on loss function
- **Novelty Detection** — MSP (Maximum Softmax Probability) for out-of-distribution detection
- **Exceptional Model Mining (EMM)** — Identify subgroups where the model fails

---

## Key Results {#key-results}

### Base Model Performance

| Model | Train BA | Val BA |
|-------|----------|--------|
| Logistic Regression | 0.8583 | 0.8555 |
| Random Forest | 0.8648 | 0.8576 |
| **XGBoost** | **0.8733** | **0.8597** |

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Test AUC-ROC = 0.9280** | Test BA = 0.8656
:::

### Selective Prediction Results

::: {.callout-tip}
**Recommended Operating Point (Coverage ≥ 80%)**

- Threshold: 0.760
- Coverage: **81.0%**
- Balanced Accuracy: **0.9083** (vs full 0.8597, +0.0486)
:::

AURC (Area Under Risk-Coverage Curve) = **0.078363** (lower is better)

Full Risk-Coverage Trade-off:

| Operating Mode | Coverage | BA |
|---------------|----------|-----|
| Conservative (BA ≥ 0.90) | 84.0% | 0.9025 |
| Balanced (BA ≥ 0.88) | 92.8% | 0.8815 |
| **High-Coverage (≥ 80%)** | **81.0%** | **0.9083** |
| Cost-Optimal (ε = 0.05) | 18.3% | 0.9390 |
| Full Model (no rejection) | 100.0% | 0.8597 |

### Cost-based Abstention Optimal Point

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**ε = 0.05 optimal point: Coverage = 18.3%, BA = 0.9390**

In the accepted 19% subset: precision = 0.9652, recall = 0.9903, F1 = 0.9776
:::

### Architecture Comparison (Tree vs Deep Learning)

| Architecture | Test BA | Test AUC |
|-------------|---------|----------|
| XGBoost | 0.8656 | 0.9280 |
| LSTM | 0.8638 | 0.9223 |
| FT-Transformer | 0.8642 | 0.9234 |

> **Conclusion**: Tree-based models perform comparably to deep learning on tabular data, supporting Grinsztajn et al. (2022). Architecture is not the key — reliability boundaries are.

### Regression Task Supplementary Analysis

| Method | MAE (min) | R² |
|--------|-----------|-----|
| ETD deviation baseline | 19.80 | 0.3871 |
| XGBoost Regressor | 18.68 | 0.4203 |

Selective Regression (Conformal coverage = 0.909):

- Top 80% confidence: MAE = 11.84 min
- Top 90% confidence: MAE = 14.12 min
- Classification vs Regression agreement: **96.8%**

---

## ETD Leakage {#etd-leakage}

### What is ETD?

**ETD (Estimated Time of Departure)** is a real-time output computed by the airline's operations system — it reflects "how much longer the airline believes this flight will take to depart."

The problem: **ETD directly encodes the answer.**

::: {.callout-warning}
## FATAL ISSUE: ETD Data Leakage

**ETD deviation correlation with arrival delay: ≈ 0.92**

This means the model is essentially learning "whatever ETD says the flight is late by, predict that amount of delay." This is not prediction — it is parroting.
:::

### ETD Subgroup Survival Analysis

The subset with ETD deviation > 5 minutes contains 347,436 flights, of which 118,033 (34.0%) still arrived on time ("survivors"):

| ETD Bin | Survival Rate (On-time) | Sample Size |
|---------|------------------------|-------------|
| 5–30 min | 45.2% | 256,905 |
| 30–60 min | 3.4% | 54,127 |
| > 60 min | 0.6% | 36,404 |

### 9 "Do-Not-Use" Slices

Gen1 discovered 9 model failure subgroups, all with BA ≤ 0.51:

::: {.callout-warning}
**All 9 slices share a common characteristic: they are all ETD deviation segments**

| Slice Condition | BA |
|----------------|-----|
| ETD deviation = late_>60 | 0.5000 |
| ETD deviation = late_30-60 | 0.5000 |
| ETD deviation = late_>60 AND aircraft = EMJ | 0.5000 |
| ETD deviation = late_30-60 AND aircraft = EMJ | 0.5000 |
| ETD deviation = late_>60 AND aircraft = CRJ | 0.5000 |
| ETD deviation = late_30-60 AND aircraft = CRJ | 0.5000 |
| ETD deviation = late_5-30 AND aircraft = EMJ | 0.5098 |
| ETD deviation = late_5-30 AND aircraft = CRJ | 0.5121 |
| ETD deviation = late_5-30 | 0.5143 |

**Not a single slice is related to weather, route, or time of day.**
:::

### Why AUC 0.928 is Fake

When a flight's ETD is already 30–60 minutes late, the model assumes it "will definitely be delayed" (all samples predicted as delay), resulting in BA = 0.5 (coin flip). This shows the model learned no useful delay patterns — it was merely relaying ETD information.

::: {.callout-warning}
**Conclusion: Gen1 is entirely invalidated**

- AUC 0.9280 is inflated — ETD directly encodes arrival delay
- All 9 "do-not-use" slices are byproducts of ETD bins
- Research question shifted from "when not to use the model" to "how to build a truly predictive model under strict conditions"
:::

:::

---

```{=html}
<script>
window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  var hash = window.location.hash.replace('#', '');
  if (!hash) return;
  var tabs = document.querySelectorAll('[data-bs-toggle="tab"], [data-bs-toggle="pill"]');
  for (var i = 0; i < tabs.length; i++) {
    var target = tabs[i].getAttribute('data-bs-target') || tabs[i].getAttribute('href') || '';
    if (target.indexOf(hash) !== -1) {
      tabs[i].click();
      break;
    }
  }
});
</script>
```
